purpose: Bootstrap module for the languages group; installs Rust via rustup (with rust-analyzer component), Go and OpenJDK+Maven via apt, and .NET via the Microsoft apt feed (best-effort) — providing compiled-language toolchains beyond Python and Node.
invariants: Rust is installed via rustup (not apt) to get the current stable toolchain with cargo, rustfmt, and clippy
Go, OpenJDK, and Maven come from Debian trixie apt — current enough and no PATH additions needed
.NET install is explicitly best-effort: a warning is logged and the group continues if the Microsoft feed is unavailable
rust-analyzer is installed as a rustup component, not a standalone binary — presence is detected via 'rustup component list --installed'
languages_rust sources ~/.cargo/env immediately after install so cargo is usable for the rest of the bootstrap run
gotchas: Gradle is deliberately omitted — Debian apt's version is 4.x (ancient); projects must use their own ./gradlew wrapper
.NET installation downloads and installs a Microsoft .deb package to register the feed, which modifies /etc/apt/sources.list.d — will linger even if dotnet is later removed
The .NET install tries dotnet-sdk-9.0 first and falls back to dotnet-sdk-8.0; if neither is in the feed, it logs a warning and skips without failing the group
languages_rust_analyzer runs independently of languages_rust so it installs/verifies even when Rust is already present from a previous run
Rust is added to PATH for the current bootstrap run by sourcing ~/.cargo/env inline — the permanent PATH update is handled by rustup's shell-rc line, not by core_write_shellrc

key_functions.name: languages_rust
key_functions.summary: Installs the Rust toolchain via the rustup installer script, skipping if cargo is already present, and sources ~/.cargo/env to activate it immediately

key_functions.name: languages_rust_analyzer
key_functions.summary: Adds the rust-analyzer rustup component if not already installed; warns and skips if cargo is absent

key_functions.name: languages_dotnet
key_functions.summary: Registers the Microsoft apt feed for the current Debian version and installs dotnet-sdk-9.0 (or 8.0 fallback) — logs a warning and exits cleanly if the feed is unavailable

key_functions.name: languages_record_manifest
key_functions.summary: Upserts manifest entries for rust, rustfmt, clippy, rust-analyzer, go, openjdk, maven, and dotnet if their binaries are present
